shell.brandSubtitle: Manage setup, transcripts, add-ons, and advanced tools from one place.
shell.providerReady: Registered and ready for guarded actions.

flash.rateLimited: Too many login attempts. Try again in {minutes} minute(s).
flash.csrfExpired: The sign-in page expired. Try again.
flash.discordNotReady: Discord admin sign-in is not ready on this host yet.
flash.discordStateExpired: The Discord sign-in attempt expired. Start again.
flash.discordCodeMissing: Discord sign-in did not return a usable code.
flash.discordAuthFailed: Discord sign-in could not be completed.
flash.adminAccessDenied: Your Discord account does not currently have admin-host access.

landing.title: Open the dashboard and keep the first step simple.
landing.subtitle: Sign in to manage setup, transcripts, add-ons, and advanced tools from one restrained dashboard surface.
landing.supportBody: Dashboard access uses Discord sign-in on this host. After sign-in, Home, Transcripts, Add-ons, and Advanced stay on the authenticated rail, while setup workspaces open from Home.

login.title: Sign in
login.headline: Sign in
login.subtitle: Use Discord sign-in with current Editor or Admin access on the admin host.
login.heroAlt: Era of the Fallen Sun dinosaur crest
login.breakglassNotice: Breakglass stays secondary, admin-host only, and disabled by default unless you explicitly enable it.

admin.title: Dashboard admin
admin.headline: Configuration editors
admin.subtitle: Use the visual editor for guided updates or switch to advanced JSON when you need direct control.

general.title: General settings
general.description: Bot token, language, status, system behavior, permissions, limits, and message delivery controls.

options.title: Ticket options
options.description: Ticket, website, and role options with round-trip-safe editing for live enum values and nested settings.

panels.title: Panels
panels.description: Panel embeds, selected options, dropdown layouts, and option descriptions for ticket entry points.

questions.title: Questions
questions.description: Question definitions, placeholders, required flags, and response length controls.

supportTeams.title: Support teams
supportTeams.description: Admin-only team ownership, role membership, assignment strategy, and route escalation bindings.

integrationProfiles.title: Integration profiles
integrationProfiles.description: Provider-owned ticket integration profiles, secret-aware settings, and option bindings.

aiAssistProfiles.title: AI assist profiles
aiAssistProfiles.description: Advisory AI assist provider profiles, local knowledge bindings, and ticket option assignments.

knowledgeSources.title: Knowledge sources
knowledgeSources.description: Local markdown and FAQ files available to AI assist profiles.

transcripts.title: Transcripts
transcripts.description: Delivery targets, transcript modes, text layouts, HTML styling, and favicon behavior.

home.title: Home
home.headline: Home
home.subtitle: Check system status and review setup from one place.
home.warningsEmptyBody: The dashboard did not detect any current runtime warnings for this snapshot.

reasons.missing_server_id: Add the server ID so Open Ticket knows which Discord server this dashboard is managing.
reasons.missing_command_mode: Enable slash commands, text commands, or both so the ticket system can actually receive requests.
reasons.missing_token: Add a bot token or enable environment-token loading before the bot can sign in.

reasons.empty: Create at least one option so members have something to open from a panel.
Create a panel after your options are ready so members can start opening tickets.

reasons.invalid_mode: Transcript delivery is enabled, but the configured mode is missing or invalid.
reasons.html_integration_unavailable: HTML transcript mode is enabled, but the transcript integration is not ready yet.

questions.title: Add intake questions when you need them

reasons.empty_optional: Questions are optional. Add them when you want users to answer prompts before a ticket is created.

reasons.daily_operations: Core setup is in place. Move into transcripts, add-ons, and advanced tools as needed.

general.label: General
general.what: Bot connection, server targeting, command modes, logs, limits, and core behavior.
general.tasks: Set the server ID, choose command modes, and provide a token or enable environment-token loading.

reasons.missing_command_mode: Enable at least one command mode.
reasons.missing_token: Add a token or enable token-from-environment.
reasons.configured: The main connection and command settings are in place.

options.label: Options
options.what: Ticket types, channels, limits, and role behavior members can use.
options.tasks: Create the first ticket option before you build panels.

reasons.configured: At least one option is ready to use.

panels.label: Panels
panels.what: The entry panels members use to open tickets.
panels.tasks: Create a panel after your options exist so members can start opening tickets.

reasons.configured: At least one panel is ready to publish.

questions.label: Questions
questions.what: Optional intake questions shown before a ticket opens.
questions.tasks: Add questions only if you want users to answer prompts before creating a ticket.

reasons.empty_optional: Questions are optional and currently empty.
reasons.configured: Questions are available to reuse across options.

transcripts.label: Transcripts
transcripts.what: Transcript delivery, destinations, and transcript styling.
transcripts.tasks: Review transcript delivery after your main ticket flow is in place.

reasons.invalid_mode: Transcript delivery is enabled, but the mode is missing or invalid.
reasons.configured: Transcript delivery is enabled with a valid mode.

configDetail.headline: {area} setup
configDetail.subtitle: See what this area controls, complete the common tasks, and use advanced tools only when you need them.
configDetail.recommendedBody: Use the visual editor for the common settings in this area. The raw editor stays available for full-fidelity JSON work.
configDetail.advancedBody: Preview JSON changes, import a draft, export the current file, restore from backup, or move into the raw editor.
configDetail.restoreEmptyBody: Backup history will appear here after the next guarded backup or apply flow.

addOns.title: Add-ons
addOns.headline: Add-ons
addOns.subtitle: Review add-on state here. Open a workbench only when you need manifest, JSON, or backup tools.
addOns.searchPlaceholder: Search id, author, or JSON file
addOns.emptyBody: The dashboard did not detect any plugins for this project root.
addOns.filteredEmptyBody: Try a plugin ID, author name, or JSON file path.

detail.subtitle: Review the add-on purpose and status first, then move into advanced tools for manifests, JSON files, backups, and diagnostics.

overview.jsonFilesDetail: Editable files discovered for this add-on.

detail.advancedBody: Manifest changes, plugin JSON assets, backups, and plugin-owned actions stay available here for experienced operators.
detail.manifestHelp: Only `enabled` and `priority` are patched. All other `plugin.json` fields stay untouched apart from formatting normalization.
detail.manifestEmptyBody: This plugin does not currently expose a readable `plugin.json` file for structured manifest editing.
detail.assetsEmptyBody: The dashboard only exposes JSON files outside `plugin.json`, `locales`, `public`, `dist`, `node_modules`, and dot-directories.
detail.backupNotePlaceholder: Optional note for this plugin snapshot
detail.backupsRestoreHelp: Restore stays file-scoped. Open an asset page to review a backup copy against the live file before apply.
detail.backupsEmptyBody: Creating a plugin backup captures `plugin.json`, every editable JSON asset, and `metadata.json` under the runtime-owned dashboard backup tree.

compatibility.title: Compatibility and dependencies
compatibility.noIssues: No additional diagnostics were detected from the merged manifest and runtime view.

detail.registryEmptyBody: This plugin section did not return any items.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.headline: System status and maintenance
advanced.subtitle: Runtime, backups, advanced JSON editors, and plugin maintenance stay available here without crowding the primary workflow.

systemStatus.title: System status
systemStatus.description: Open the live runtime snapshot and guarded automation tools.

backups.title: Backups & restore
backups.description: Review runtime-owned backups and restore previews.

transcriptConfig.title: Transcript settings
transcriptConfig.description: Open transcript setup or the transcript workspace without losing the beginner-first primary nav.

security.title: Security workspace
security.description: Manage non-secret host routing and RBAC entries without exposing credentials or low-level auth tuning.

systemStatus.title: System status
systemStatus.description: Open the live runtime snapshot and the guarded automation tools that work against the running bot.

backups.title: Backups and restore
backups.description: Review runtime-owned backup inventory and restore previews before you apply changes.

editors.title: Advanced JSON editors
editors.description: Open raw JSON for a specific managed file without changing the POST contracts behind the visual editors.

items.general: Edit the raw General configuration file.
items.options: Edit the raw Options configuration file.
items.panels: Edit the raw Panels configuration file.
items.questions: Edit the raw Questions configuration file.
items.integration-profiles: Edit the raw Integration Profiles configuration file.
items.transcripts: Edit the raw Transcripts configuration file.

plugins.title: Plugin maintenance
plugins.description: Open plugin inventory and plugin-specific workbenches for manifest, backup, and JSON asset work.

diagnostics.title: Runtime diagnostics links
diagnostics.description: Use these links when you need health, runtime, or backup diagnostics without returning to the default beginner workflow.

security.title: Security workspace
security.headline: Review host routing and RBAC
security.subtitle: Only non-secret host routing and RBAC fields are editable here. Secrets stay read-only, and runtime backup plus audit records are created before apply.

summary.adminHostDetail: Canonical URL for the admin host.
summary.viewerHostDetail: Canonical URL for the viewer host.
summary.ownersDetail: Seeded owner overrides for admin access.
summary.warningsDetail: Current dashboard exposure and readiness checks.

routing.title: Host routing
routing.description: Set the canonical admin host, canonical viewer host, and trusted reverse-proxy hop count.

rbac.title: RBAC
rbac.description: Map owner IDs plus reviewer, editor, and admin role or user IDs.

breakglass.title: Breakglass
breakglass.description: Enable or disable breakglass recovery without exposing the stored password hash.
breakglass.help: The emergency password hash stays read-only. Only the enabled flag is editable here.

secrets.title: Secret readiness
secrets.description: These statuses are read-only. Secret values never appear in the browser or the auth store.

fields.listHelp: Enter one ID per line or separate values with commas.

discord.title: Discord OAuth
discord.readyDetail: Discord client id and client secret are both present.
discord.missingDetail: Discord client id or client secret is missing.

session.title: Session secret
session.readyDetail: A session secret is present and meets the minimum length guidance.
session.missingDetail: The session secret is missing, too short, or still using a placeholder.

breakglass.title: Breakglass hash
breakglass.readyDetail: Breakglass is enabled and a password hash is configured.
breakglass.missingDetail: Breakglass is enabled but no password hash is configured.

savebar.title: Apply guarded security changes
savebar.body: Saving creates a runtime backup, a workspace record, and an auth-store audit row before the plugin config is updated.

messages.preApplyBackup: Pre-apply backup for dashboard security workspace

editor.title: Advanced JSON editor
editor.subtitle: Edit the raw JSON directly. Invalid JSON will be rejected.
editor.saveShortcut: Press Ctrl+S or Cmd+S to save.
editor.commonBody: Start with the fields most admins usually change.
editor.guidedBody: Use the visual editor for the settings most admins change often, then move into deeper controls only when the routine path is not enough.
editor.rawBody: Open the raw editor when you need exact file-level control, imports, or a full-fidelity review of the managed JSON.
editor.advancedBody: Open the remaining config surface only when you need expert control.

advancedTools.title: Advanced tools
advancedTools.body: Keep raw JSON, export, backup, review, and restore close without making them the primary path.
advancedTools.backupBody: Capture the current managed file before a broader change.
advancedTools.reviewBody: Paste or adjust a draft here, then open the guarded diff review before anything writes to disk.
advancedTools.restoreBody: Preview a backup copy against the live file before you apply it.
advancedTools.restoreEmptyBody: Backup history appears here after the next guarded backup or apply flow.

sections.connectionBody: Set token, server, language, and command entry.
sections.advancedBody: Keep rare flags in collapsed groups.
sections.channelTopicBody: Ticket facts written into the topic.

fields.globalAdminsHelp: Store Discord role IDs as a JSON array of quoted strings, for example ["123456789012345678"].
fields.globalAdminsRecoveredWarning: The saved value matched the known legacy line-split corruption pattern. Review the repaired role-ID JSON below, then save to rewrite it cleanly.
fields.globalAdminsInvalidSavedWarning: The saved value is invalid and could not be safely repaired. Review the raw JSON below, convert it to a JSON array of quoted Discord role IDs, then save.
fields.globalAdminsErrorInvalidJson: Enter valid JSON for this field. Use a JSON array of quoted Discord role IDs.
fields.globalAdminsErrorNotArray: Enter a JSON array of quoted Discord role IDs.
fields.globalAdminsErrorNumber: Discord role IDs must be quoted JSON strings. Numeric JSON IDs are rejected to avoid snowflake precision loss.
fields.globalAdminsErrorEmptyString: Remove empty entries from the role-ID array before saving.
fields.globalAdminsErrorNonString: Every global admin entry must be a quoted Discord role ID string.
fields.globalAdminsErrorInvalidRoleId: Every global admin entry must be a Discord role ID with 17 to 20 digits.

options.title: Options configuration
options.subtitle: Manage ticket, website, and role options.
options.emptyBody: Create your first option to populate the panel editors.

workspace.inventoryBody: Choose an option or start a draft.
workspace.editorBody: Edit the selected option with questions and panel usage in view.
workspace.positionBody: Saved order still controls panel entry order.
workspace.panelCountBody: Panel references block ID changes and delete.
workspace.identityBody: Set the name, ID, and button label first.
workspace.dependencyBody: Question assignments and panel references stay visible here.
workspace.assignedQuestionsBody: These questions will be asked when this option is selected.
workspace.assignedQuestionsEmpty: No questions are assigned to this option.
workspace.referencingPanelsBody: These panels currently include this option.
workspace.referencingPanelsEmpty: No panels currently reference this option.
workspace.referenceWarningBody: This option is still live on one or more panels. Remove those panel references before changing its ID or deleting it.
workspace.ticketBody: Configure ticket channels, automation, transcripts, and question assignment.
workspace.ticketChannelBody: Set the ticket naming and category routing.
workspace.ticketAutomationBody: Keep inactivity, limits, and cooldown controls together.
workspace.workflowBody: Configure creator close requests and staff-controlled awaiting-user reminders.
workspace.integrationBody: Bind this ticket option to generic provider profiles.
workspace.questionPickerBody: Use the structured picker so coverage stays visible while you edit.
workspace.questionPickerEmptyBody: Add questions in the Questions workspace before assigning them to ticket options here.
workspace.supportRoutingBody: Bind ticket ownership to one support team and limit valid escalation targets.
workspace.transcriptRoutingBody: Choose whether this option inherits the default transcript route or uses explicit channel IDs.
workspace.ticketAdvancedBody: Keep low-frequency ticket controls and channel metadata together.
workspace.messagesBody: Use raw JSON only when you need nested embed, field, or ping control.
workspace.websiteBody: Keep the destination URL accurate and the button copy clear.
workspace.roleBody: Keep the role list, mode, and remove-on-add behavior together.

fields.name: Name
fields.allowBlacklisted: Allow blacklisted users to create this option
fields.overflowCategoriesHelp: Enter ordered category IDs as a comma-separated list, newline-separated list, or JSON array. The first value mirrors the legacy backup category.
fields.escalationTargetsHelp: Enter comma-separated, newline-separated, or JSON-array ticket option IDs. Targets must use the same transport and have a support team.
fields.transcriptChannelsHelp: Enter comma-separated, newline-separated, or JSON-array channel IDs. Leaving this empty while inheritance is off disables transcript channel posting for this option.
fields.transcriptRoutingInheritedHelp: This option inherits the default transcript channel from the Transcripts workspace when channel delivery is enabled there.
fields.transcriptRoutingOverrideHelp: This option sends transcript-ready posts only to the explicit channel IDs listed here, even if the global transcript channel toggle is off.
fields.claimedCategoryHelp: Use a JSON array of {"user","category"} objects.
fields.autocloseOnLeave: Close when the ticket creator leaves
fields.autodeleteOnLeave: Delete when the ticket creator leaves
fields.messageJsonHelp: Use JSON to edit the full nested message config, including embeds, fields, and ping settings.

flash.validationUrl: Website options need a valid URL.
flash.validationClaimedCategory: Claimed category mappings must be a valid JSON array of user/category objects.
flash.validationMessageJson: Advanced message settings must be valid JSON objects.
flash.validationRoutingTargets: Choose an existing support team and valid escalation target option IDs.
flash.validationAiAssistProfile: Choose an existing AI assist profile.
flash.validationWorkflowHours: Awaiting-user timeout hours must be greater than reminder hours.

supportTeams.title: Support teams configuration
supportTeams.subtitle: Manage admin-only support teams for routing, assignment, and escalation.
supportTeams.emptyBody: Create a support team before binding ticket options to managed ownership.

workspace.referencingOptionsEmpty: No ticket options currently reference this support team.

fields.name: Name
fields.roleIdsHelp: Enter comma-separated, newline-separated, or JSON-array Discord role IDs. Duplicate role IDs are ignored.

flash.validationRoles: Add at least one support-team role ID.

panels.title: Panels configuration
panels.subtitle: Build ticket panels.
panels.emptyBody: Create a panel after adding one or more options.

workspace.inventoryBody: Select a panel or start a new draft.
workspace.editorBody: Edit the selected panel with option selection and preview feedback in view.
workspace.positionBody: Saved order still controls entry order.
workspace.modeBody: Switch between buttons and dropdown mode here.
workspace.optionCountBody: Selected options stay visible in the picker and preview.
workspace.identityBody: Set the ID, name, entry mode, and member copy first.
workspace.pickerBody: Choose options with emoji, type, and description still visible.
workspace.pickerEmptyBody: Create options in the Options workspace before building panels here.
workspace.previewBody: Keep the member-facing result visible while you edit.
workspace.selectedOptionsBody: These options publish with this panel.
workspace.selectedOptionsEmpty: No options are selected for this panel yet.
workspace.embedBody: Keep the main embed title, description, and color visible before opening deeper embed fields.
workspace.advancedBody: Open low-frequency embed fields and description layout switches here.

flash.validationDropdownOptions: Dropdown panels can only include ticket options.
flash.validationUnknownOptions: Panels can only include existing option IDs.
flash.validationLayout: Choose a valid options description layout.
flash.dropdownHint: Dropdown panels only support ticket options.

questions.title: Questions configuration
questions.subtitle: Manage reusable intake questions.
questions.emptyBody: Create a question to reuse it across ticket options.

workspace.usedByOptionsEmpty: No options currently reference this question.
workspace.referenceWarningBody: This question is still used by one or more options. Deleting it or changing its ID will be blocked until those option references are removed.

flash.validationIdFormat: Question IDs may only contain letters, numbers, and supported accented characters.
flash.validationIdLength: Question IDs must be between 3 and 40 characters.
flash.validationNameLength: Question names must be between 3 and 45 characters.
flash.validationPlaceholderLength: Question placeholders must be at most 100 characters.
flash.validationMinimum: Minimum length must stay between 0 and 1024.
flash.validationMaximum: Maximum length must stay between 1 and 1024.
flash.validationLength: Minimum length cannot exceed maximum length.

page.headline: Tickets
page.subtitle: Search live tickets, open detail records, and run guarded ticket actions from the workbench.
page.workspaceBody: Use the list for search, filtering, pagination, navigation, and guarded bulk actions. Rich operator workflows stay on ticket detail pages.
page.filtersBody: Filter the live ticket inventory without mutating ticket state.
page.emptyBody: Try clearing filters or wait for the runtime to publish ticket inventory.

bulk.title: Bulk actions
bulk.help: Bulk actions evaluate every selected ticket independently. Locked, unsupported, or ineligible tickets are skipped.
bulk.unsupported: Bulk ticket actions are unavailable in the current dashboard runtime.
bulk.invalidAction: That bulk ticket action is not supported.
bulk.result: Bulk action complete: {succeeded} succeeded, {skipped} skipped, {failed} failed.

filters.searchPlaceholder: Ticket, channel, creator, team, or assignee
filters.moreBody: Narrow by team, assignee, option, panel, creator, or page size.

detail.headline: Ticket {id}
detail.subtitle: Inspect ticket state, route ownership, transport metadata, participants, provider locks, and guarded runtime actions.
detail.unavailableBody: The dashboard could not load this ticket from the Open Ticket runtime.
detail.participantsEmptyBody: The runtime did not return participant labels for this ticket.
detail.aiAssistPromptPlaceholder: Enter the FAQ question or optional reply instructions

telemetry.title: Telemetry
telemetry.noRatingsBody: The latest feedback session did not include rating answers.
telemetry.unavailableBody: This dashboard runtime is not exposing ticket telemetry reads right now.

claim.title: Claim ticket
claim.body: Mark yourself as the active staff owner.

unclaim.title: Unclaim ticket
unclaim.body: Clear the current claim without changing the route.

assign.title: Assign staff
assign.body: Assign eligible staff from the owning support team.

escalate.title: Escalate route
escalate.body: Move this ticket to a configured same-transport escalation target.

move.title: Move ticket
move.body: Reroute within the same transport and owning support team.

transfer.title: Transfer creator
transfer.body: Set a different current ticket creator through the Open Ticket transfer action.

add_participant.title: Add participant
add_participant.body: Add a user participant to this ticket.

remove_participant.title: Remove participant
remove_participant.body: Remove a user participant without removing the current creator.

set_priority.title: Set priority
set_priority.body: Update the stored ticket priority and refresh ticket presentation.

set_topic.title: Set topic
set_topic.body: Update the stored ticket topic using the transport-aware Open Ticket action.

approve_close_request.title: Approve close request
approve_close_request.body: Close this ticket through the pending requester close request.

dismiss_close_request.title: Dismiss close request
dismiss_close_request.body: Clear the pending close request without closing this ticket.

set_awaiting_user.title: Set awaiting user
set_awaiting_user.body: Mark this ticket as waiting for requester activity.

clear_awaiting_user.title: Clear awaiting user
clear_awaiting_user.body: Clear the waiting marker after requester or staff follow-up.

close.title: Close ticket
close.body: Close the ticket through the Open Ticket runtime action path.

reopen.title: Reopen ticket
reopen.body: Reopen the ticket through the Open Ticket runtime action path.

refresh.title: Refresh ticket
refresh.body: Reload dashboard detail state without mutating ticket persistence.

warnings.creatorTransfer: Original applicant authority remains with {originalApplicant}; the current creator is {currentCreator}.

actionResults.requestIncomplete: The ticket action request is incomplete.
actionResults.missingTicket: Ticket is missing or no longer tracked.
actionResults.runtimeContextMissing: The Open Ticket runtime could not resolve the ticket, channel, guild, or actor.
actionResults.assignMissingAssignee: Choose an assignee before assigning this ticket.
actionResults.assignIneligibleAssignee: Selected assignee is not eligible for the owning support team.
actionResults.assignClaimedByOther: This ticket is currently claimed by another staff member. Unclaim it before assigning a different staff member.
actionResults.escalateMissingTarget: Choose a route-scoped same-transport escalation target before escalating this ticket.
actionResults.escalateInvalidTarget: Choose a valid escalation target before escalating this ticket.
actionResults.moveMissingTarget: Choose a same-owner same-transport move target before moving this ticket.
actionResults.moveInvalidTarget: Choose a valid move target before moving this ticket.
actionResults.transferMissingCreator: Choose a different eligible creator before transferring this ticket.
actionResults.participantInvalidUser: Choose a valid user participant before updating this ticket.
actionResults.participantAlreadyPresent: Selected user is already a participant.
actionResults.participantNotPresent: Selected user is not a participant.
actionResults.participantCreatorRemoveDenied: The current ticket creator cannot be removed as a participant.
actionResults.priorityInvalid: Choose a valid priority before updating this ticket.
actionResults.priorityUnconfigured: Choose a configured Open Ticket priority before updating this ticket.
actionResults.topicMissing: Enter a ticket topic before updating this ticket.
actionResults.aiAssistUnavailable: AI assist is unavailable for this ticket.
actionResults.aiAssistDenied: Open Ticket denied this AI assist request.

availability.writesUnavailable: Dashboard runtime ticket writes are unavailable.
availability.genericUnavailable: This action is unavailable for the current ticket state.
availability.ticketAlreadyClaimedOrNotOpen: Ticket is already claimed or not open.
availability.runtimeContextMissing: The Open Ticket runtime could not resolve the ticket, channel, guild, or actor.
availability.busyWorkflow: Ticket is busy in an existing Open Ticket workflow.
availability.noOwningSupportTeam: This ticket route has no owning support team.
availability.noEligibleSupportMembers: This ticket route has no eligible support-team members.
availability.noEscalationTargets: This ticket route has no escalation targets.
availability.noSameTransportEscalationTargets: This ticket route has no same-transport escalation targets.
availability.noSameOwnerMoveTargets: This ticket route has no same-owner same-transport move targets. Use escalate for ownership-transfer routes.
availability.noEligibleNewCreators: No eligible new creator users are available.
availability.noEligibleAddUsers: No eligible users are available to add.
availability.noParticipantsToRemove: No user participants are available to remove.
availability.workflowDisabled: This ticket workflow is disabled for the ticket option.
availability.closeRequestPending: A close request is already pending.
availability.awaitingUserActive: This ticket is already awaiting user response.
availability.awaitingUserMissing: This ticket is not awaiting user response.

deferredActions.pinUnpinRename: Pin, unpin, and freeform rename remain Discord-only actions in this packet.

summary.openedDetail: Tickets opened in the selected UTC window
summary.historyUnavailableDetail: Unavailable until transcript analytics history is available

telemetry.noRateDenominator: No eligible records in the selected window.
telemetry.unavailableWarning: Ticket telemetry reads are unavailable, so feedback and reopen metrics are unavailable.
telemetry.truncatedWarning: Ticket telemetry was truncated, so feedback and reopen metrics are unavailable.
telemetry.scanCeilingWarning: Ticket telemetry exceeded the dashboard scan ceiling, so feedback and reopen metrics are unavailable.
telemetry.readFailureWarning: Ticket telemetry could not be read, so feedback and reopen metrics are unavailable.
telemetry.feedbackTriggeredDetail: Feedback sessions triggered in the selected window
telemetry.feedbackDeliveryFailedDetail: Feedback sessions that could not be delivered
telemetry.reopenedTicketsDetail: Tickets reopened in the selected window
telemetry.unavailableTable: Ticket telemetry is unavailable for this table.
telemetry.emptyFeedback: No feedback sessions matched this window.
telemetry.emptyRatings: No rating answers matched this window.
telemetry.emptyReopens: No closed or reopened tickets matched this window.
telemetry.emptyReopenedBacklog: No reopened open tickets matched the current filters.

page.headline: Analytics
page.subtitle: Measure live backlog and SLA performance from runtime tickets and archived transcript metadata.
page.workspaceBody: Backlog uses current live tickets. Cohort and SLA metrics use transcript-backed ticket metadata when the history reader is available.
page.historyUnavailable: Transcript analytics history is unavailable, so cohort and SLA metrics are unavailable.
page.emptyCohort: No tickets opened in this window.
page.filtersBody: Use UTC windows and optional route filters without mutating ticket state.

transcripts.title: Transcripts configuration
transcripts.subtitle: Control transcript delivery defaults and preview the locked Discord-default appearance.
transcripts.htmlModeWarning: HTML transcript mode is enabled, but the transcript integration is not ready. {reason}

preview.title: HTML preview
preview.body: HTML transcripts now use the locked Discord-default dark appearance. Refresh the preview iframe to confirm the current render before saving other transcript settings.
preview.unavailableService: Preview is unavailable until the transcript service is healthy and connected to the dashboard runtime.
preview.unavailableUnsupported: This transcript service build does not expose style presets or HTML preview rendering yet.
preview.unavailableError: The transcript preview could not be rendered right now. You can still save transcript settings normally.

discord-classic.label: Discord Default
discord-classic.description: Locked Discord dark theme used for every HTML transcript.

midnight-cyan.label: Midnight Cyan
midnight-cyan.description: Cool blue contrast with a brighter operational accent.

ember-slate.label: Ember Slate
ember-slate.description: Warmer dark neutrals with orange emphasis for alert-heavy archives.

forest-ledger.label: Forest Ledger
forest-ledger.description: Muted greens tuned for softer long-form reading.

editor.introBody: Review delivery defaults first, then tune text output and preview the locked Discord-default HTML appearance before you save. Ticket option overrides live in the Options workspace.
editor.advancedBody: Open the embed-only transcript options after the main delivery choices are set.
editor.appearanceLockedBody: Background, header, stats, accent, image, and favicon styling are no longer configurable here. Legacy theme values are ignored and overwritten with the locked Discord-default palette the next time you save this form.

fields.generalEnableCreatorDM: Send a DM to the creator
fields.generalEnableActiveAdminDM: Send a DM to the active admin
fields.generalEnableEveryAdminDM: Send a DM to every admin
fields.generalChannelHelp: Ticket options that keep transcript inheritance enabled use this channel when transcript channel delivery is turned on.

preview.title: HTML preview
preview.body: HTML transcripts now use the locked Discord-default dark appearance. Refresh the preview iframe to confirm the current render before saving other transcript settings.
preview.unavailableService: Preview is unavailable until the transcript service is healthy and connected to the dashboard runtime.
preview.unavailableUnsupported: This transcript service build does not expose style presets or HTML preview rendering yet.
preview.unavailableError: The transcript preview could not be rendered right now. You can still save transcript settings normally.

discord-classic.label: Discord Default
discord-classic.description: Locked Discord dark theme used for every HTML transcript.

midnight-cyan.label: Midnight Cyan
midnight-cyan.description: Cool blue contrast with a brighter operational accent.

ember-slate.label: Ember Slate
ember-slate.description: Warmer dark neutrals with orange emphasis for alert-heavy archives.

forest-ledger.label: Forest Ledger
forest-ledger.description: Muted greens tuned for softer long-form reading.

access.privateModeBody: Canonical transcript links now point to the dashboard viewer and require Discord sign-in from the transcript creator or a stored participant. Whitelist review submit also depends on a dedicated OT-guild transcript archive lane and a ready viewer host.

viewer.notReady: Dashboard transcript viewer is not ready.

portal.title: My Transcripts
portal.headline: My Transcripts
portal.subtitle: Only transcript archives you can open right now appear on the viewer host.

summary.tierDetail: Reviewer, Editor, or Admin when live RBAC resolves.

portal.emptyBody: Only creator-owned archives and current recorded-admin staff assignments appear here.
portal.failClosedNotice: Current guild membership could not be confirmed, so the portal stays empty until live access checks succeed again.
portal.ownerOverrideNotice: Owner override does not populate My Transcripts. Use the admin host transcript workspace for global inventory.

login.title: Transcript viewer
login.headline: Sign in to view this transcript
login.subtitle: Private transcripts require Discord sign-in and a current guild-membership check before the archive can load.

errors.state: The Discord viewer sign-in state expired or did not match. Please try again.
errors.code: Discord did not return a valid viewer sign-in code. Please try again.
errors.auth: Discord viewer sign-in could not be completed. Please try again.
errors.access: Your Discord account does not currently have viewer-host access.

page.headline: Review transcript archives
page.subtitle: Search transcript records, inspect archived details, and open advanced transcript actions only on the detail page.
page.workspaceBody: Review transcript archives here. Single-record actions stay on transcript detail pages, and bulk tools stay with the table below.

filters.summary: Search and narrow the transcript record list.
filters.moreBody: IDs, date range, sort, and page size.
filters.searchPlaceholder: Transcript id, ticket id, channel id, slug, or reason

page.emptyUnavailableBody: Records appear here after the transcript runtime reconnects.
page.emptyBody: Try clearing the search, widening the status filter, or wait for the next transcript archive to finish.

bulk.title: Bulk actions
bulk.reasonPlaceholder: Optional reason for revoke or delete actions
bulk.help: Current page only. Eligibility still depends on each transcript record.
bulk.revokeConfirm: Revoke every selected active transcript link now?
bulk.deleteConfirm: Delete every selected eligible transcript archive now?
bulk.unsupported: This transcript service build does not expose bulk actions or export downloads yet.

operations.title: Operations overview
operations.unavailableUnsupported: This transcript service build does not expose retention preview or integrity summary reads yet.
operations.unavailableError: The transcript service is healthy for archive management, but the read-only operations overview could not be loaded.
operations.unavailableCompact: This overview fills in when the transcript service can answer read-only archive checks again.

retention.title: Retention overview
retention.startupDetail: Uses the configured startup sweep behavior.
retention.windowsDetail: Configured in the transcript service retention policy.
retention.disabledBody: Retention preview is available, but automatic archive sweeping is currently turned off.
retention.emptyBody: The transcript service did not report any current retention candidates for the configured windows.

detail.unavailableBody: The transcript service is not ready, so the dashboard cannot load transcript detail or mutation controls right now.
detail.subtitleUnavailable: The dashboard could not resolve the transcript service, so transcript detail and mutation controls are disabled until the integration recovers.
detail.headline: Transcript {id}
detail.subtitle: Review transcript status, current link access, participant coverage, mirrored assets, and guarded revoke/reissue/delete actions backed by the transcript service.
detail.noPublicUrl: This transcript does not currently expose an active public URL.

integrity.title: Integrity
integrity.unavailableUnsupported: This transcript service build does not expose per-transcript integrity scans yet.
integrity.unavailableError: The transcript service could not load integrity data for this transcript right now.
integrity.unavailableMissing: The transcript service returned no integrity report for this transcript.
integrity.emptyBody: The transcript service reported a healthy integrity scan with no issues for this archive.

events.title: Event history
events.unavailableUnsupported: This transcript service build does not expose transcript event history yet.
events.unavailableError: The transcript service could not load transcript event history right now.
events.emptyBody: The transcript service did not return any recorded events for this transcript.

detail.linksEmptyBody: The transcript service did not return any stored links for this transcript.
detail.participantsEmptyBody: The transcript service did not return any stored participants for this transcript.
detail.assetsEmptyBody: The transcript service did not return any mirrored asset metadata for this transcript.

operations.exportHelp: Download a staged ZIP export for this transcript without changing its current public link state.
operations.exportUnavailable: This transcript service build does not expose direct transcript export downloads yet.
operations.revokePlaceholder: Optional reason for revoking the active link
operations.revokeHelp: Use this when the current public link should stop working but the archive itself should remain available for later review.
operations.revokeConfirm: Revoke the active transcript link now?
operations.reissuePlaceholder: Optional reason for generating a new slug
operations.reissueHelp: Use this when the archive stays valid but the public URL should rotate to a fresh slug.
operations.reissueConfirm: Create a new public transcript link and supersede the old one?
operations.deletePlaceholder: Optional reason for deleting this archive
operations.deleteHelp: Use this only when the archive itself must be removed and every public link should stop working immediately.
operations.deleteConfirm: Delete this transcript archive and invalidate every link?

systemStatusPage.title: System status
systemStatusPage.headline: Review live runtime health
systemStatusPage.subtitle: Check the runtime snapshot, health signals, and guarded automation tools from the running dashboard.
systemStatusPage.snapshotBody: Inspect the live runtime snapshot first, then use maintenance actions only when the runtime looks safe.
systemStatusPage.maintenanceBody: These guarded actions run against the live runtime and return you to this Advanced page after completion.
systemStatusPage.warningsEmptyBody: The runtime snapshot did not report any degraded warnings.

backupsPage.title: Backups & restore
backupsPage.headline: Review backups before you restore anything
backupsPage.subtitle: Use this page for runtime-owned backup inventory, restore previews, and verification guidance.
backupsPage.inventoryBody: Review runtime-owned backups here before you open a restore preview or apply any recovery step.
backupsPage.inventoryEmptyBody: Create a backup from a guarded setup or plugin flow to populate this list.

pluginAsset.subtitle: Edit this plugin-owned JSON file inline, review changes before apply, export the current file, or restore a file-scoped snapshot from a plugin backup.
pluginAsset.help: Applying this reviewed JSON writes directly to the plugin asset on disk. Restart is required unless the plugin exposes an explicit live action for the affected behavior.
pluginAsset.backupsEmptyBody: Create a plugin backup from the plugin workbench to restore this asset from a runtime-owned snapshot.

routeMessages.priorityInvalid: Priority must be a valid number.
routeMessages.invalidExportFormat: Export format must be JSON or CSV.
routeMessages.exportFailed: The export could not be generated right now.
routeMessages.reviewTokenExpired: The review token is missing or expired.
routeMessages.configReviewSubtitle: The diff below compares the current managed config to the reviewed candidate. Confirm only when the impact summary matches your intent.
routeMessages.configReviewConfirm: Apply this reviewed config to the source-of-truth file now?
routeMessages.configReviewErrorSubtitle: Fix the draft below and review again.
routeMessages.configRestoreSubtitle: This review compares the live config against the selected backup artifact. Confirm to restore the backup contents into the source-of-truth file.
routeMessages.manifestReviewSubtitle: This review patches only `enabled` and `priority` in `plugin.json`. Applying the reviewed manifest writes to disk and requires a bot restart to take effect.
routeMessages.manifestReviewConfirm: Apply this reviewed manifest to plugin.json now?
routeMessages.manifestReviewErrorSubtitle: Fix the manifest draft below and review again.
routeMessages.manifestApplySuccess: Applied plugin.json. Restart required for manifest changes to take effect.
routeMessages.pluginAssetReviewSubtitle: The diff below compares the live plugin asset against the reviewed candidate. Applying the reviewed JSON writes to disk and requires a bot restart unless the plugin exposes an explicit live action.
routeMessages.pluginAssetReviewConfirm: Apply this reviewed JSON to the plugin asset now?
routeMessages.pluginAssetReviewErrorSubtitle: Fix the draft below and review again.
routeMessages.pluginAssetApplySuccess: Applied {path}. Restart required unless the plugin exposes an explicit live action.
routeMessages.pluginAssetRestoreSubtitle: This restore preview compares the live plugin asset against the selected backup copy. Restoring stays file-scoped and does not roll the entire plugin back.
routeMessages.pluginAssetRestoreConfirm: Restore this backup copy into the live plugin asset now?
